title: Semiconductor sector faces broad valuation reset
summary: Analysts across Wall Street are reassessing semiconductor valuations after a prolonged AI-driven rally. NVDA, AMD, and INTC all saw pressure as price-to-earnings multiples came under scrutiny.

title: AI trade expectations moderate after Fed commentary
summary: Federal Reserve officials signaled a cautious stance on rate cuts, dampening risk appetite. AI-linked equities including NVDA saw selling pressure as investors rotated to defensive positions.

title: Analyst downgrades NVDA on near-term supply chain concerns
summary: A Morgan Stanley analyst lowered their near-term price target for NVDA citing potential supply chain bottlenecks for next-gen GPU components, though the long-term thesis remains intact.

title: Apple faces regulatory headwinds in EU
summary: The European Union opened a new antitrust investigation into Apple's App Store practices, adding regulatory uncertainty to the stock's near-term outlook.

title: Tesla delivery numbers disappoint for Q1
summary: Tesla's preliminary Q1 delivery figures came in below analyst consensus, raising concerns about demand softness in key markets including China and Europe.

title: Broad market pullback on inflation data
summary: The S&P 500 declined 1.2% after CPI data came in slightly above expectations, reigniting concerns about the Fed's rate path and compressing equity multiples across sectors.

title: Microsoft Azure AI revenue surges 35% YoY
summary: Microsoft reported Azure cloud revenue growth of 35% year-over-year, driven by enterprise AI workloads and Copilot adoption. The beat pushed MSFT shares up 4% in after-hours trading and reinforced the company's position as a top AI infrastructure play.

title: Google Gemini Ultra gains enterprise traction
summary: Alphabet's Gemini Ultra model is seeing accelerating enterprise adoption, with over 2,000 companies integrating it into workflows. Analysts raised price targets citing Google's improving AI monetization trajectory and Search resilience.

title: Meta's Llama 4 open-source release reshapes AI landscape
summary: Meta released Llama 4, its most capable open-source model to date, outperforming several closed-source competitors on key benchmarks. The release strengthens Meta's developer ecosystem and could pressure margins at AI API providers.

title: Amazon AWS expands AI chip lineup with Trainium 3
summary: Amazon Web Services unveiled Trainium 3, its third-generation custom AI training chip, promising 40% better price-performance than NVIDIA H100s for large model training. The announcement signals AWS's intent to reduce dependency on NVDA hardware.

title: Bitcoin consolidates near $85,000 amid ETF inflow slowdown
summary: Bitcoin is trading in a tight range near $85,000 as spot ETF inflows moderate from their January peak. On-chain data shows long-term holders are not selling, suggesting the consolidation may be a healthy pause before the next leg up.

title: JPMorgan raises recession probability to 40%
summary: JPMorgan's chief economist raised the 12-month US recession probability to 40%, citing persistent inflation, tightening credit conditions, and slowing consumer spending. The bank recommends increasing allocation to defensive sectors and investment-grade bonds.

title: Gold hits all-time high as safe-haven demand surges
summary: Gold prices surpassed $3,100 per ounce for the first time, driven by central bank buying, geopolitical uncertainty, and investors seeking inflation hedges. Analysts at Goldman Sachs raised their year-end gold target to $3,300.

title: Fed holds rates steady, signals two cuts in 2026
summary: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate at 4.25–4.50% at its March meeting, maintaining a cautious stance. The updated dot plot signals two 25bps cuts in 2026, fewer than the market had priced in, causing a brief equity selloff before markets stabilized.
